title: Pad Thai / Noodle Sauce on Polyester
timeEstimate: 30–60 minutes; 4-component combo + polyester heat-lock + oleophilic 2 Dawn passes
stainChemistry: Pad Thai on polyester is the four-component combo (tamarind tannin + fish-sauce protein + caramelizing sugar + peanut/chili oil) plus polyester's oleophilic + heat-sensitive complications. Polyester grabs the peanut and chili oil tight (Dawn × 2 typical) and locks color under heat. The recurring traps stack: hot water (caramelizes sugar + cooks protein + sets tannin + locks color into polyester all at once), alkaline cleaners as first step (darken tannin permanently), and hot dryer (yellows polyester over time with the residue). Order is the same as cotton (sugar → protein → oil → tannin → peroxide) but cool-throughout and no hot wash finish.
whyThisWorks: Long cold flush dissolves sugar before caramelization. Cool enzyme handles fish-sauce protein. Dawn × 2 strips peanut and chili oil polyester grabbed. Vinegar disrupts tamarind tannin. 3% peroxide on white polyester finishes residual color. Cool wash plus AIR-DRY locks nothing. The honest cap on dyed polyester: faint shadow may remain.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): pad-thai is tamarind tannin polyphenols + fish-sauce proteins + sucrose/palm sugar + peanut + chili oil with capsanthin pigment. Polyester is hydrophobic and oleophilic. Pro uses cold flush + protein spotter + NSD oil-strip × 2 + acid-side tannin spotter + 3% H₂O₂ for residual chromophore.
defaultAssumption: Thai food / noodle takeout spill on a polyester shirt or blend. Fresh = real shot with full sequence. Hot-dried = often a sticky brown shadow with locked-in color. Performance / specialty / dry-clean-only polyester = pro.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Scrape solid noodles and sauce with plastic spoon edge. Flat-edge work only — never rub.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold running water, white catch towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Hold the BACK of the spot under cold running water 90+ sec. Cold only — heat caramelizes sugar + cooks protein + sets tannin + locks color into polyester.

spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean Versatile / Biz / Tide Plus per label, COOL water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dissolve enzyme pretreat in COOL water. Apply to spot, keep damp under a cloth, 20 min. Cool only.
Generous Dawn. Soft toothbrush 10 min. Polyester is oleophilic — extended dwell needed. Cool damp-cloth rinse.
Touch with fingertip. Oily feel = repeat Dawn. 2 passes typical on polyester for noodle oils.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 1:1 white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Vinegar + cool water on damp cloth, 5 min. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before peroxide.
WHITE polyester only. Hidden test on dyed. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. NEVER mix with chlorine, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners.
Cool machine wash. AIR-DRY only. NEVER hot dryer until cleared — locks residue into polyester.

steps: 2. Long cold back-flush 90+ sec — dissolve sugar.
3. Enzyme pretreat in COOL water, 20 min — protein FIRST.
4. Dawn pretreat × 2 — polyester needs 2 oil-strip passes.
5. White vinegar 1:1 cool 5 min — tannin acid, rinse before peroxide.
6. 3% H₂O₂ damp cloth on white polyester only.
7. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. No softener, no dryer until cleared.
products: Enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile / Biz / Tide Plus / liquid Protein Spotter)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets every component on polyester
warnings: ORDER MATTERS — sugar flush, then protein, then oil × 2, then tannin acid, then peroxide
NEVER hot water — caramelizes sugar + cooks protein + sets tannin + locks color into polyester
NEVER alkaline cleaners as first step — DARKEN tannin permanently
Polyester is oleophilic — Dawn × 2 typical for noodle oils
Hidden test on dyed polyester before peroxide

homeSolutions.agent: Four Stains + Polyester Heat-Lock — Order Matters
homeSolutions.instruction: Pad Thai on polyester combines four components (sugar, protein, oil, tannin) plus polyester being oleophilic and heat-sensitive. Order is the same as cotton (sugar → protein → oil → tannin → peroxide) but COOL throughout and no hot wash finish — polyester locks color under heat. Three hard rules: NO hot water (caramelizes sugar + cooks protein + sets tannin + locks color all at once), NO alkaline cleaners as first step (darken tannin permanently), NO hot dryer until cleared (yellows polyester with the residue).
Scrape solid noodles, sauce, and peanuts off with a plastic spoon edge. Flat-edge work only.

homeSolutions.agent: Long Cold Water Back-Flush — Ninety Seconds Plus
homeSolutions.instruction: Turn the polyester inside out. Hold the BACK of the spot under cold running water 90+ seconds. Cold only — heat caramelizes the sugar AND cooks the fish-sauce protein AND sets the tannin AND drives color into polyester.

homeSolutions.agent: Enzyme Pretreat — Cool Water, Protein FIRST
homeSolutions.instruction: Dissolve enzyme pretreat in COOL water per label. Apply to spot, keep damp under a cloth, 20 minutes. Cool only — heat damages enzymes AND sets the protein.
Generous Dawn dish soap. Work in with a soft toothbrush for 10 minutes. Polyester is oleophilic — extended dwell needed. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

homeSolutions.agent: Repeat Dawn — Polyester Needs Two Passes
homeSolutions.instruction: Touch with a clean fingertip. Still oily? Repeat Dawn. Polyester typically needs 2 Dawn passes for the peanut and chili oil in pad-thai.
1 part white vinegar + 1 part cool water on damp cloth, 5 minutes. Disrupts tamarind tannin. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide.
WHITE polyester only: 3% peroxide on a damp cloth, press 5 to 10 minutes. Hidden-area test on dyed polyester first.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners — toxic fumes.

homeSolutions.instruction: Cool machine wash. AIR-DRY only. NEVER hot dryer until cleared — locks residue into polyester. Honest cap on dyed polyester: faint shadow may remain.

steps: 1. NO hot water. NO alkaline cleaners as first step. NO hot dryer until cleared.
3. Long cold back-flush 90+ sec — dissolve sugar.
4. Enzyme pretreat in COOL water, 20 min — protein FIRST.
6. Diluted white vinegar 5 min — tannin acid, rinse before peroxide.
7. 3% peroxide damp cloth on white polyester only.
8. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. No dryer until cleared.
products: Enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz, Tide Plus)
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot water or hot dryer was used (often locks every component). Stop if alkaline cleaner was used as first step. Stop on performance / specialty polyester. Stop after 3 home cycles plateau.
warnings: ORDER MATTERS — sugar / protein / oil × 2 / tannin acid / peroxide in sequence
NEVER hot water — locks every component on polyester
NEVER alkaline cleaners as first step — DARKEN tannin
Hidden test on dyed polyester before peroxide

neverDo: Never hot water on pad-thai + polyester
Never alkaline cleaners as first step on tannin
Never reverse the order — sugar FIRST, then protein, then oil × 2, then tannin acid, then peroxide
Never mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + OxiClean
fiberSensitivities: Polyester is oleophilic — needs 2 Dawn passes for noodle oils
Polyester locks color under heat — strict cool discipline
Dyed polyester: hidden test mandatory; honest cap is partial removal

materialWarnings: Order matters — 5-step sequence in cool water
Polyester loves noodle oils — Dawn × 2 mandatory
No hot water / no hot dryer — polyester locks color
Honest cap on dyed polyester for residual shadow

escalation.when: Hot water or hot dryer was used (often locks every component on polyester). Alkaline cleaner used as first step. Performance / specialty polyester. 3 home cycles plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Pad Thai / noodle sauce on [white / dyed / athletic / specialty] polyester, age [fresh / hours / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [scrape + long cold back-flush + cool enzyme + Dawn × 2 + vinegar + 3% peroxide on white + cool wash + air-dry]. Order followed. No hot, no alkaline. [Residual shadow / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with combination-stain (sugar + protein + oil + tannin) chemistry

contextQuestions: Pad Thai, lo mein, drunken noodles, or other Asian sauce?
White, dyed, athletic, or specialty polyester?
Has hot water or hot dryer been used?
Has alkaline cleaner been used as first step?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Same four-component template as cotton with polyester's oleophilic + heat-lock complications. Sugar caramelization and the polyester heat-lock together make this a tougher case than the cotton variant. Sunny location for sun-drying after wash adds finishing power on residual color (UV photodegrades capsanthin from chili oil).
